name: Mountain Pose
steps: Stand with feet together, big toes touching, heels slightly apart.
Distribute weight evenly across both feet.
Engage thighs, draw belly in, and roll shoulders back.
Arms rest by sides with palms facing forward.
Align head over spine, looking straight ahead.

name: Downward-Facing Dog
steps: Start on hands and knees, hands slightly forward of shoulders.
Tuck toes and lift knees away from the floor.
Extend tailbone up toward the ceiling, pushing hips back.
Press heels toward the mat, keeping legs straight or slightly bent.
Press hands firmly into the mat, fingers spread.
common_mistakes: Placing too much weight on hands

name: Plank Pose
steps: Start in Downward Dog, then push torso forward until arms are vertical.
Keep shoulders aligned directly over wrists.
Keep body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage core and thighs to prevent hips from sagging.

name: Triangle Pose
steps: Stand with feet wide apart. Turn right foot out 90 degrees.
Extend arms to the sides, parallel to the floor.
Hinge at the right hip, extending torso over right leg.
Lower right hand to shin, ankle, or floor, and reach left hand up.
Gaze up toward left hand if comfortable.

name: Warrior I
steps: Stand in Tadasana, step left foot back 3-4 feet and turn it out 45 degrees.
Align right heel with left heel.
Bend right knee to 90 degrees, directly over ankle.
Raise arms overhead, parallel or palms touching.
Turn pelvis forward toward front edge of mat.

name: Warrior II
steps: Stand feet wide apart, turn right foot out 90 degrees and left foot slightly in.
Bend right knee to 90 degrees over right ankle.
Extend arms out to the sides, parallel to the floor.
Turn head and gaze over right fingers.
Keep torso upright, not leaning forward.

name: Tree Pose
steps: Stand in Tadasana. Shift weight onto left leg.
Bend right knee, placing sole of right foot against left inner thigh or calf.
Never place foot directly on the knee joint.
Bring hands to prayer position at chest or extend them overhead.
Find a steady point to focus your gaze.
common_mistakes: Allowing standing hip to splay outward

name: Cobra Pose
steps: Lie face down on floor, tops of feet flat on mat.
Place hands under shoulders, elbows close to ribs.
Press tops of feet and thighs firmly into mat.
Inhale, press hands down to lift chest off floor.
Keep shoulders down away from ears.
common_mistakes: Straightening arms completely and locking elbows

name: Child's Pose
steps: Kneel on floor, touch big toes together, sit on heels.
Separate knees about hip-width apart.
Fold torso forward over thighs, resting forehead on mat.
Lay arms forward palms down, or backward along sides.
common_mistakes: Lifting hips too high off heels

name: Bridge Pose
steps: Lie on back, bend knees, place feet flat on floor hip-width apart.
Exhale and press feet and arms into floor, lifting hips.
Keep thighs and feet parallel.
Clasp hands under lower back, stretching arms toward feet.

name: Chair Pose
steps: Stand in Tadasana, inhale and raise arms overhead.
Exhale, bend knees, and lower hips as if sitting in a chair.
Try to keep thighs parallel to the floor.
Keep back straight and chest lifted.

name: Goddess Pose
steps: Stand with feet about 3 feet apart. Turn toes out 45 degrees.
Bend knees deeply, aiming for thighs parallel to ground.
Bend elbows 90 degrees with fingers pointing up (cactus arms).
Keep spine tall, tailbone tucked.
common_mistakes: Sinking hips too low without core control
precautions: Recent hip, knee, or shoulder injury

name: Corpse Pose
steps: Lie flat on back, feet wider than hip-width apart.
Let feet roll open. Extend arms slightly out from sides, palms up.
Close eyes, soften face, breathe naturally.
Relax entire body completely, letting go of thoughts.
precautions: Severe back pain (bend knees or support with a bolster)

name: Boat Pose
benefits: Strengthens abdomen, hip flexors, and spine
steps: Sit with knees bent, feet flat on floor.
Lean back slightly, keeping spine straight.
Lift feet off floor, keeping knees bent or straightening legs.
Extend arms forward, parallel to floor.
Balance on sit bones, creating a V shape.

name: Camel Pose
steps: Kneel on floor, knees hip-width apart. Tops of feet flat on floor.
Place hands on lower back, fingers pointing down.
Lean back slowly, lifting chest toward ceiling.
If comfortable, reach hands back to touch heels.
Keep thighs perpendicular to floor.
common_mistakes: Letting head drop completely without control

name: Eagle Pose
steps: Stand in Tadasana, bend knees slightly.
Lift right leg, wrap right thigh over left, hook right foot behind left calf.
Cross left elbow inside right, wrap forearms, palms touch.
Lift elbows to shoulder height and press palms together.
common_mistakes: Losing balance and leaning torso forward

name: Fish Pose
steps: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Slide hands under buttocks, palms down.
Squeeze elbows close together.
Inhale, press elbows into floor, arch chest up.
Lower head back, resting crown lightly on floor.
common_mistakes: Putting too much weight on head

name: Garland Pose
steps: Squat with feet close together, heels flat if possible.
Separate thighs wider than torso.
Press elbows against inner knees, bring palms to prayer position.
Lengthen spine and lift chest.
common_mistakes: Heels lifting off floor (support with towel if needed)

name: Half Moon Pose
steps: From Triangle Pose, place left hand on left hip. Bend right knee.
Step right hand forward 12 inches on the floor.
Lift left leg parallel to floor, straightening right leg.
Rotate chest open to left, raising left arm up.

name: King Dancer Pose
steps: Stand in Tadasana. Shift weight onto right foot.
Bend left knee, reach back with left hand to clasp inner left ankle.
Lift left foot up and back, away from torso.
Reach right arm forward, parallel to floor.
common_mistakes: Splaying left knee out to side
precautions: Recent knee, ankle, or hip injury

name: Locust Pose
benefits: Strengthens spine, buttocks, and back of arms
steps: Lie face down, arms along sides, palms down.
Exhale and lift head, chest, arms, and legs off floor.
Gaze forward or slightly down to keep neck long.
Keep arms parallel, palms facing each other.

name: Reclining Bound Angle
steps: Lie on back, bend knees, place feet flat on floor.
Bring soles of feet together, let knees drop open to sides.
Rest arms comfortably, palms up, by sides.
Close eyes and breathe deeply.
precautions: Groin or knee injury (support knees with blocks)

name: Staff Pose
steps: Sit on floor, legs straight out in front.
Flex feet, pulling toes toward torso.
Place palms flat on floor next to hips, fingers pointing forward.
Lengthen spine, lift chest, tuck chin slightly.

name: Upward-Facing Dog
steps: Lie face down, tops of feet on mat. Hands beside waist.
Inhale, press hands and tops of feet firmly into floor.
Straighten arms, lifting torso and thighs off mat.
Keep shoulders directly over wrists, rolling shoulders back.

name: Wheel Pose
benefits: Strengthens arms, wrists, spine, and legs
steps: Lie on back, bend knees, feet flat. Place hands beside head, fingers toward shoulders.
Press feet and hands into floor, lifting hips and chest.
Rest crown of head lightly on floor briefly, then press up fully.
Straighten arms and legs as much as possible.

name: Cow Pose
steps: Start on hands and knees (tabletop position).
Inhale, drop belly toward floor.
Lift chest and chin, looking up toward ceiling.
Draw shoulders away from ears.

name: Cat Pose
steps: Start on hands and knees (tabletop position).
Exhale, round spine toward ceiling.
Release head down, looking toward navel.
Press hands firmly into mat, broadening shoulders.

name: Easy Pose
steps: Sit cross-legged on floor or blanket.
Keep spine erect, shoulders relaxed.
Rest hands on knees, palms up or down.
Close eyes and focus on breathing.

name: Seated Forward Bend
steps: Sit in Dandasana. Inhale and reach arms overhead.
Exhale, fold forward from hips, keeping spine long.
Reach for shins, ankles, or feet.
Rest forehead on legs if possible.

name: Plow Pose
steps: Lie on back, lift legs overhead, then lift hips.
Lower toes to floor behind head, legs straight.
Clasp hands on floor behind back, extending arms.
Keep neck straight, do not turn head.
